      },
      "score": 0.3616754412651062
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "│   └── Markdown instructions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "└── Bundled Resources (optional)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    ├── scripts/    - Executable code for deterministic/repetitive tasks"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "    ├── references/ - Docs loaded into context as needed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "    └── assets/     - Files used in output (templates, icons, fonts)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "#### Progressive Disclosure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "Skills use a three-level loading system:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "1. **Metadata** (name + description) - Always in context (~100 words)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "2. **SKILL.md body** - In context whenever skill triggers (\u003c500 lines ideal)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "3. **Bundled resources** - As needed (unlimited, scripts can execute without loading)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "These word counts are approximate and you can feel free to go longer if needed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "**Key patterns:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "- Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines; if you're approaching this limit, add an additional layer of hierarchy along with clear pointers about where the model using the skill should go next to follow up."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "- Reference files clearly from SKILL.md with guidance on when to read them"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "- For large reference files (\u003e300 lines), include a table of contents"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "**Domain organization**: When a skill supports multiple domains/frameworks, organize by variant:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "cloud-deploy/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "├── SKILL.md (workflow + selection)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "└── references/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "    ├── aws.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "    ├── gcp.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "    └── azure.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "Claude reads only the relevant reference file."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "#### Principle of Lack of Surprise"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "This goes without saying, but skills must not contain malware, exploit code, or any content that could compromise system security. A skill's contents should not surprise the user in their intent if described. Don't go along with requests to create misleading skills or skills designed to facilitate unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or other malicious activities. Things like a \"roleplay as an XYZ\" are OK though."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "#### Writing Patterns"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "Prefer using the imperative form in instructions."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "**Defining output formats** - You can do it like this:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;markdown"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "## Report structure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "ALWAYS use this exact template:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "# [Title]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "## Executive summary"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "## Key findings"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "## Recommendations"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 128
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 78
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.356775164604187
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "3. What's the expected output format?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "4. Should we set up test cases to verify the skill works? Skills with objectively verifiable outputs (file transforms, data extraction, code generation, fixed workflow steps) benefit from test cases. Skills with subjective outputs (writing style, art) often don't need them. Suggest the appropriate default based on the skill type, but let the user decide."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "### Interview and Research"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "Proactively ask questions about edge cases, input/output formats, example files, success criteria, and dependencies. Wait to write test prompts until you've got this part ironed out."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "Check available MCPs - if useful for research (searching docs, finding similar skills, looking up best practices), research in parallel via subagents if available, otherwise inline. Come prepared with context to reduce burden on the user."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "### Write the SKILL.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "Based on the user interview, fill in these components:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "- **name**: Skill identifier"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "- **description**: When to trigger, what it does. This is the primary triggering mechanism - include both what the skill does AND specific contexts for when to use it. All \"when to use\" info goes here, not in the body. Note: currently Claude has a tendency to \"undertrigger\" skills -- to not use them when they'd be useful. To combat this, please make the skill descriptions a little bit \"pushy\". So for instance, instead of \"How to build a simple fast dashboard to display internal Anthropic data.\", you might write \"How to build a simple fast dashboard to display internal Anthropic data. Make sure to use this skill whenever the user mentions dashboards, data visualization, internal metrics, or wants to display any kind of company data, even if they don't explicitly ask for a 'dashboard.'\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "- **compatibility**: Required tools, dependencies (optional, rarely needed)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "- **the rest of the skill :)**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "### Skill Writing Guide"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "#### Anatomy of a Skill"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "skill-name/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "├── SKILL.md (required)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "│   ├── YAML frontmatter (name, description required)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "│   └── Markdown instructions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "└── Bundled Resources (optional)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    "
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 4,
            "line": 80
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 52
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.35440540313720703
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "name: canvas-design"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "description: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. You should use this skill when the user asks to create a poster, piece of art, design, or other static piece. Create original visual designs, never copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "These are instructions for creating design philosophies - aesthetic movements that are then EXPRESSED VISUALLY. Output only .md files, .pdf files, and .png files."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "Complete this in two steps:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "1. Design Philosophy Creation (.md file)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "2. Express by creating it on a canvas (.pdf file or .png file)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "First, undertake this task:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "## DESIGN PHILOSOPHY CREATION"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "To begin, create a VISUAL PHILOSOPHY (not layouts or templates) that will be interpreted through:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "- Form, space, color, composition"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- Images, graphics, shapes, patterns"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- Minimal text as visual accent"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "### THE CRITICAL UNDERSTANDING"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- What is received: Some subtle input or instructions by the user that should be taken into account, but used as a foundation; it should not constrain creative freedom."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- What is created: A design philosophy/aesthetic movement."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- What happens next: Then, the same version receives the philosophy and EXPRESSES IT VISUALLY - creating artifacts that are 90% visual design, 10% essential text."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "Consider this approach:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "- Write a manifesto for an art movement"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "- The next phase involves making the artwork"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "The philosophy must emphasize: Visual expression. Spatial communication. Artistic interpretation. Minimal words."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "### HOW TO GENERATE A VISUAL PHILOSOPHY"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "**Name the movement** (1-2 words): \"Brutalist Joy\" / \"Chromatic Silence\" / \"Metabolist Dreams\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "**Articulate the philosophy** (4-6 paragraphs - concise but complete):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "To capture the VISUAL essence, express how the philosophy manifests through:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "- Space and form"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "- Color and material"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "- Scale and rhythm"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "- Composition and balance"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "- Visual hierarchy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "**CRITICAL GUIDELINES:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "- **Avoid redundancy**: Each design aspect should be mentioned once. Avoid repeating points about color theory, spatial relationships, or typographic principles unless adding new depth."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "- **Emphasize craftsmanship REPEATEDLY**: The philosophy MUST stress multiple times that the final work should appear as though it took countless hours to create, was labored over with care, and comes from someone at the absolute top of their field. This framing is essential - repeat phrases like \"meticulously crafted,\" \"the product of deep expertise,\" \"painstaking attention,\" \"master-level execution.\""
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 48
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/canvas-design/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.35234034061431885
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "**If no integrations are detected and in Claude.ai or Claude app:** Suggest they can enable connectors in their Claude settings to allow pulling context from messaging apps and document storage directly."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "Inform them clarifying questions will be asked once they've done their initial dump."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "**During context gathering:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "- If user mentions team channels or shared documents:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "  - If integrations available: Inform them the content will be read now, then use the appropriate integration"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "  - If integrations not available: Explain lack of access. Suggest they enable connectors in Claude settings, or paste the relevant content directly."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "- If user mentions entities/projects that are unknown:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "  - Ask if connected tools should be searched to learn more"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "  - Wait for user confirmation before searching"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "- As user provides context, track what's being learned and what's still unclear"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "**Asking clarifying questions:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "When user signals they've done their initial dump (or after substantial context provided), ask clarifying questions to ensure understanding:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "Generate 5-10 numbered questions based on gaps in the context."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "Inform them they can use shorthand to answer (e.g., \"1: yes, 2: see #channel, 3: no because backwards compat\"), link to more docs, point to channels to read, or just keep info-dumping. Whatever's most efficient for them."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "**Exit condition:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "Sufficient context has been gathered when questions show understanding - when edge cases and trade-offs can be asked about without needing basics explained."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "**Transition:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "Ask if there's any more context they want to provide at this stage, or if it's time to move on to drafting the document."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "If user wants to add more, let them. When ready, proceed to Stage 2."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "## Stage 2: Refinement \u0026 Structure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "**Goal:** Build the document section by section through brainstorming, curation, and iterative refinement."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "**Instructions to user:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "Explain that the document will be built section by section. For each section:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "1. Clarifying questions will be asked about what to include"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "2. 5-20 options will be brainstormed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "3. User will indicate what to keep/remove/combine"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 112
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 70
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/doc-coauthoring/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.34200319647789
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "name: algorithmic-art"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "description: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "Algorithmic philosophies are computational aesthetic movements that are then expressed through code. Output .md files (philosophy), .html files (interactive viewer), and .js files (generative algorithms)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "This happens in two steps:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "1. Algorithmic Philosophy Creation (.md file)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "2. Express by creating p5.js generative art (.html + .js files)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "First, undertake this task:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "## ALGORITHMIC PHILOSOPHY CREATION"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "To begin, create an ALGORITHMIC PHILOSOPHY (not static images or templates) that will be interpreted through:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "- Computational processes, emergent behavior, mathematical beauty"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- Seeded randomness, noise fields, organic systems"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- Particles, flows, fields, forces"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "- Parametric variation and controlled chaos"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "### THE CRITICAL UNDERSTANDING"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- What is received: Some subtle input or instructions by the user to take into account, but use as a foundation; it should not constrain creative freedom."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- What is created: An algorithmic philosophy/generative aesthetic movement."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "- What happens next: The same version receives the philosophy and EXPRESSES IT IN CODE - creating p5.js sketches that are 90% algorithmic generation, 10% essential parameters."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Consider this approach:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "- Write a manifesto for a generative art movement"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "- The next phase involves writing the algorithm that brings it to life"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "The philosophy must emphasize: Algorithmic expression. Emergent behavior. Computational beauty. Seeded variation."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "### HOW TO GENERATE AN ALGORITHMIC PHILOSOPHY"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "**Name the movement** (1-2 words): \"Organic Turbulence\" / \"Quantum Harmonics\" / \"Emergent Stillness\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "**Articulate the philosophy** (4-6 paragraphs - concise but complete):"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 39
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/algorithmic-art/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3407664895057678
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "name: frontend-design"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "This skill guides creation of distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic \"AI slop\" aesthetics. Implement real working code with exceptional attention to aesthetic details and creative choices."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "The user provides frontend requirements: a component, page, application, or interface to build. They may include context about the purpose, audience, or technical constraints."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "## Design Thinking"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "Before coding, understand the context and commit to a BOLD aesthetic direction:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "- **Purpose**: What problem does this interface solve? Who uses it?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "- **Tone**: Pick an extreme: brutally minimal, maximalist chaos, retro-futuristic, organic/natural, luxury/refined, playful/toy-like, editorial/magazine, brutalist/raw, art deco/geometric, soft/pastel, industrial/utilitarian, etc. There are so many flavors to choose from. Use these for inspiration but design one that is true to the aesthetic direction."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "- **Constraints**: Technical requirements (framework, performance, accessibility)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "- **Differentiation**: What makes this UNFORGETTABLE? What's the one thing someone will remember?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "**CRITICAL**: Choose a clear conceptual direction and execute it with precision. Bold maximalism and refined minimalism both work - the key is intentionality, not intensity."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "Then implement working code (HTML/CSS/JS, React, Vue, etc.) that is:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- Production-grade and functional"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- Visually striking and memorable"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- Cohesive with a clear aesthetic point-of-view"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- Meticulously refined in every detail"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "## Frontend Aesthetics Guidelines"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 28
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.33938050270080566
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "name: mcp-builder"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "# MCP Server Development Guide"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "## Overview"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "Create MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. The quality of an MCP server is measured by how well it enables LLMs to accomplish real-world tasks."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "# Process"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "## 🚀 High-Level Workflow"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "Creating a high-quality MCP server involves four main phases:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "### Phase 1: Deep Research and Planning"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "#### 1.1 Understand Modern MCP Design"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "**API Coverage vs. Workflow Tools:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "Balance comprehensive API endpoint coverage with specialized workflow tools. Workflow tools can be more convenient for specific tasks, while comprehensive coverage gives agents flexibility to compose operations. Performance varies by client—some clients benefit from code execution that combines basic tools, while others work better with higher-level workflows. When uncertain, prioritize comprehensive API coverage."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "**Tool Naming and Discoverability:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "Clear, descriptive tool names help agents find the right tools quickly. Use consistent prefixes (e.g., &#96;github_create_issue&#96;, &#96;github_list_repos&#96;) and action-oriented naming."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "**Context Management:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "Agents benefit from concise tool descriptions and the ability to filter/paginate results. Design tools that return focused, relevant data. Some clients support code execution which can help agents filter and process data efficiently."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "**Actionable Error Messages:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "Error messages should guide agents toward solutions with specific suggestions and next steps."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "#### 1.2 Study MCP Protocol Documentation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "**Navigate the MCP specification:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "Start with the sitemap to find relevant pages: &#96;https://modelcontextprotocol.io/sitemap.xml&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "Then fetch specific pages with &#96;.md&#96; suffix for markdown format (e.g., &#96;https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/draft.md&#96;)."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 43
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/mcp-builder/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.33278319239616394
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "text": "### Step 1: Spawn all runs (with-skill AND baseline) in the same turn"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "For each test case, spawn two subagents in the same turn — one with the skill, one without. This is important: don't spawn the with-skill runs first and then come back for baselines later. Launch everything at once so it all finishes around the same time."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": "**With-skill run:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "Execute this task:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,
            "text": "- Skill path: \u003cpath-to-skill\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "- Task: \u003ceval prompt\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179,
            "text": "- Input files: \u003ceval files if any, or \"none\"\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "- Save outputs to: \u003cworkspace\u003e/iteration-\u003cN\u003e/eval-\u003cID\u003e/with_skill/outputs/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181,
            "text": "- Outputs to save: \u003cwhat the user cares about — e.g., \"the .docx file\", \"the final CSV\"\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184,
            "text": "**Baseline run** (same prompt, but the baseline depends on context):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185,
            "text": "- **Creating a new skill**: no skill at all. Same prompt, no skill path, save to &#96;without_skill/outputs/&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "- **Improving an existing skill**: the old version. Before editing, snapshot the skill (&#96;cp -r \u003cskill-path\u003e \u003cworkspace\u003e/skill-snapshot/&#96;), then point the baseline subagent at the snapshot. Save to &#96;old_skill/outputs/&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "Write an &#96;eval_metadata.json&#96; for each test case (assertions can be empty for now). Give each eval a descriptive name based on what it's testing — not just \"eval-0\". Use this name for the directory too. If this iteration uses new or modified eval prompts, create these files for each new eval directory — don't assume they carry over from previous iterations."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "  \"eval_id\": 0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "  \"eval_name\": \"descriptive-name-here\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "  \"prompt\": \"The user's task prompt\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195,
            "text": "  \"assertions\": []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199,
            "text": "### Step 2: While runs are in progress, draft assertions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "Don't just wait for the runs to finish — you can use this time productively. Draft quantitative assertions for each test case and explain them to the user. If assertions already exist in &#96;evals/evals.json&#96;, review them and explain what they check."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203,
            "text": "Good assertions are objectively verifiable and have descriptive names — they should read clearly in the benchmark viewer so someone glancing at the results immediately understands what each one checks. Subjective skills (writing style, design quality) are better evaluated qualitatively — don't force assertions onto things that need human judgment."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205,
            "text": "Update the &#96;eval_metadata.json&#96; files and &#96;evals/evals.json&#96; with the assertions once drafted. Also explain to the user what they'll see in the viewer — both the qualitative outputs and the quantitative benchmark."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 205
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 167
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.326886385679245
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 334,
            "text": "- All parameters must have UI controls"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 335,
            "text": "- Regenerate, Reset, Download buttons must work"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 336,
            "text": "- Keep Anthropic branding (UI styling, not art colors)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 337
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 338,
            "text": "### USING THE ARTIFACT"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 339
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 340,
            "text": "The HTML artifact works immediately:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 341,
            "text": "1. **In claude.ai**: Displayed as an interactive artifact - runs instantly"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 342,
            "text": "2. **As a file**: Save and open in any browser - no server needed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 343,
            "text": "3. **Sharing**: Send the HTML file - it's completely self-contained"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 344
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 345,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 346
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 347,
            "text": "## VARIATIONS \u0026 EXPLORATION"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 348
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 349,
            "text": "The artifact includes seed navigation by default (prev/next/random buttons), allowing users to explore variations without creating multiple files. If the user wants specific variations highlighted:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 350
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 351,
            "text": "- Include seed presets (buttons for \"Variation 1: Seed 42\", \"Variation 2: Seed 127\", etc.)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 352,
            "text": "- Add a \"Gallery Mode\" that shows thumbnails of multiple seeds side-by-side"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 353,
            "text": "- All within the same single artifact"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 354
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 355,
            "text": "This is like creating a series of prints from the same plate - the algorithm is consistent, but each seed reveals different facets of its potential. The interactive nature means users discover their own favorites by exploring the seed space."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 356
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 357,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 358
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 359,
            "text": "## THE CREATIVE PROCESS"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 360
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 361,
            "text": "**User request** → **Algorithmic philosophy** → **Implementation**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 362
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 363,
            "text": "Each request is unique. The process involves:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 364
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 365,
            "text": "1. **Interpret the user's intent** - What aesthetic is being sought?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 366,
            "text": "2. **Create an algorithmic philosophy** (4-6 paragraphs) describing the computational approach"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 367,
            "text": "3. **Implement it in code** - Build the algorithm that expresses this philosophy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 368,
            "text": "4. **Design appropriate parameters** - What should be tunable?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 369,
            "text": "5. **Build matching UI controls** - Sliders/inputs for those parameters"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 370
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 371,
            "text": "**The constants**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 372,
            "text": "- Anthropic branding (colors, fonts, layout)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 373,
            "text": "- Seed navigation (always present)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 374,
            "text": "- Self-contained HTML artifact"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 375
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 376,
            "text": "**Everything else is variable**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 377,
            "text": "- The algorithm itself"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 378,
            "text": "- The parameters"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 379,
            "text": "- The UI controls"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 380,
            "text": "- The visual outcome"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 381
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 382,
            "text": "To achieve the best results, trust creativity and let the philosophy guide the implementation."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 383
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 384,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 385
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 386,
            "text": "## RESOURCES"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 387
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 388,
            "text": "This skill includes helpful templates and documentation:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 389
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 333
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.claude/skills/algorithmic-art/SKILL.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3258576989173889
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 12720,
            "text": "Looking at the available skills from the rules - I see skills like algorithmic-art, brand-guidelines, doc-coauthoring, etc. There's no explicit \"brainstorm\" skill listed. The user might mean they want me to brainstorm/think through approaches rather than use a specific skill. I'll interpret this as: think through multiple strategies to get xtask-todo-lib coverage from 21.77% to 95%+."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12721
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12722,
            "text": "From the earlier conversation we know:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12723,
            "text": "1. xtask-todo-lib has both a library and a binary (cargo_devshell) in crates/todo"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12724,
            "text": "2. The library has good coverage when measured alone (~95%+)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12725,
            "text": "3. The binary (crates/todo/src/bin/cargo_devshell/) has 0% coverage - lots of files: command.rs, completion.rs, main.rs, parser.rs, repl.rs, serialization.rs, todo_io.rs, vfs.rs"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12726,
            "text": "4. The user explicitly said NOT to use --exclude-files to ignore the binary - they want real coverage of all source"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12727
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12728,
            "text": "So to get from 21.77% to 95%+, we need to add tests that exercise the cargo_devshell binary code. That's a substantial amount of code (hundreds of lines across many modules)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12729
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12730,
            "text": "Let me brainstorm approaches:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12731
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12732,
            "text": "**Option A: Unit tests inside the binary crate**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12733,
            "text": "- The binary is in src/bin/cargo_devshell/ - it's a multi-file binary. We could add #[cfg(test)] modules in each file or a tests/ directory that tests the library parts. But binaries typically don't have their own test target - when you &#96;cargo test -p xtask-todo-lib&#96;, it runs the lib's tests. The binary is only built/run when you run the binary. So we need to either:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12734,
            "text": "  - Move testable logic into the lib and call it from the binary (so lib tests cover it)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12735,
            "text": "  - Or add integration tests that run the binary and/or invoke its code"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12736
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12737,
            "text": "**Option B: Expose binary logic as a library**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12738,
            "text": "- Refactor cargo_devshell so that the core logic lives in the lib (or in a shared module that's part of the package but compiled for both). Then the existing lib tests (or new tests in the lib) can cover that logic. The binary becomes a thin CLI wrapper. This way we don't \"exclude\" any source - we just move it so the same source is exercised by tests."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12739
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12740,
            "text": "**Option C: Integration tests that run the binary**"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 12740
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 12718
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.specstory/history/2026-03-11_01-15Z-coverage.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.19535154104232788
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# 头脑风暴：将 xtask-todo-lib 覆盖率提升到 95% 以上"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "## 现状"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "- 包内既有**库**（list、store、repeat 等）也有 **cargo-devshell** 二进制。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "- &#96;cargo test -p xtask-todo-lib&#96; 只运行**库的测试**；测试二进制只链接库，**不会执行** binary 的代码。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "- 因此 binary 下所有代码覆盖率为 0%，整体约 21.77%。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "## 方案对比"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "| 方案 | 做法 | 优点 | 缺点 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "|------|------|------|------|"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "| **A. 排除 binary** | &#96;--exclude-files \"crates/todo/src/bin/*\"&#96; | 实现简单，库可达 95%+ | 已明确不允许忽略源码 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "| **B. 集成测试启动 binary** | 在 tests/ 里 &#96;Command&#96; 启动 cargo-devshell，传 stdin 断言 stdout | 不挪代码 | Tarpaulin 通常只统计当前进程，子进程覆盖可能不计入 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "| **C. 将 binary 逻辑迁入 lib** | 把 parser/serialization/vfs/command/repl 等迁到 &#96;src/devshell/&#96;，binary 只保留薄封装并调用 &#96;devshell::run_main()&#96; | 同份代码被测试二进制链接并执行，覆盖率可靠 | 需要一次重构和导入修正 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "## 选定方案：C（逻辑迁入 lib）"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- 不排除任何源码，只是把“同一份源码”放到 lib 下，由测试二进制链接并执行。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- 在 lib 内为 parser、vfs、serialization、command、repl 等加单元/集成式测试，即可把整包覆盖率拉到 95%+。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "## 实施步骤"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "1. 在 lib 中新增 &#96;devshell&#96; 模块，将 &#96;src/bin/cargo_devshell/&#96; 下除 &#96;main.rs&#96; 外的模块迁入 &#96;src/devshell/&#96;，并统一为 &#96;super::&#96; / &#96;crate::&#96; 导入。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "2. 在 devshell 中实现 &#96;run_main()&#96;，把当前 main 的“解析参数 → 加载 VFS → 运行 REPL”逻辑放进去；binary 的 main 只调用 &#96;xtask_todo_lib::devshell::run_main()&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "3. 为 parser、vfs、serialization、command、repl 增加单元测试（含错误分支）；用 mock  stdin/stdout 测试 REPL 与命令执行路径。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "4. 运行 &#96;cargo tarpaulin -p xtask-todo-lib&#96; 验证整体覆盖率。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "**当前结果**：已将覆盖率从 21.77% 提升到 **~90%**（devshell 逻辑迁入 lib，并做可测性重构）。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "### 可测性重构（便于继续提覆盖率）"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 32
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/coverage-brainstorm.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.17830675840377808
    },
    {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12933
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12934,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12935,
            "text": "\u003c/tool-use\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12936
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12937,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12938
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12939,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12940
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12941,
            "text": "Now I have a good picture. Let me write a brainstorm document and then implement the approach: move devshell code into the lib so it gets linked into the test binary, then add unit tests. I'll create a doc first with the brainstorm, then implement."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12942
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12943,
            "text": "Actually the user asked to use \"ĺ¤´čéŁć´ćč˝\" (brainstorm skill) - I don't have a specific skill named that. I'll do a structured brainstorm and then implement the best approach."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12944
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12945,
            "text": "Brainstorm summary:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12946,
            "text": "1. **Root cause**: Coverage is 21% because &#96;cargo test&#96; only runs the lib's test binary; the cargo_devshell binary is never executed, so its code has 0% coverage."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12947,
            "text": "2. **Option A â Exclude binary**: User already said no."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12948,
            "text": "3. **Option B â Integration test that spawns binary**: Tarpaulin typically only instruments the test process; spawning the binary as a child may not attribute coverage to it. Risky."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12949,
            "text": "4. **Option C â Move binary logic into lib**: Move parser, serialization, vfs, command, todo_io, repl, completion into the lib (e.g. as a &#96;devshell&#96; module). The binary becomes a thin &#96;fn main() { devshell::run_main() }&#96;. Then add unit tests in the lib that call into devshell (parser::parse, vfs methods, serialization round-trip, command execution with mock VFS, repl with mock stdin). This way the same source is compiled into the test binary and gets coverage. No source is excluded; we just reorganize."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12950
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12951,
            "text": "I'll go with Option C. Implementation steps:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 12951
          },

            "line": 12911
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# SpecStory Artifacts Directory"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "This directory is automatically created and maintained by the SpecStory extension to preserve your AI chat history."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "## What's Here?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "- &#96;.specstory/history&#96;: Contains auto-saved markdown files of your AI coding sessions (default path, configurable in &#96;specstory.outputPath&#96; setting)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "    - Each file represents a separate AI chat session"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "    - If you enable auto-save, files are automatically updated as you work"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    - You can enable/disable the auto-save feature in the SpecStory settings, it is disabled by default"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "- &#96;.specstory/.project.json&#96;: Contains the persistent project identity for the current workspace"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    - This file is only present if you enable AI rules derivation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "    - This is used to provide consistent project identity of your project, even as the workspace is moved or renamed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "- &#96;.specstory/ai_rules_backups&#96;: Contains backups of the &#96;.cursor/rules/derived-cursor-rules.mdc&#96; or the &#96;.github/copilot-instructions.md&#96; file"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    - Backups are automatically created each time the &#96;.cursor/rules/derived-cursor-rules.mdc&#96; or the &#96;.github/copilot-instructions.md&#96; file is updated"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    - You can enable/disable the AI Rules derivation feature in the SpecStory settings, it is disabled by default"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "- &#96;.specstory/.gitignore&#96;: Contains directives to exclude non-essential contents of the &#96;.specstory&#96; directory from version control"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    - Add &#96;/history&#96; to exclude the auto-saved chat history from version control"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Valuable Uses"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- Capture: Keep your context window up-to-date when starting new Chat/Composer sessions via @ references"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- Search: For previous prompts and code snippets "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- Learn: Meta-analyze your patterns and learn from your past experiences"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- Derive: Keep the AI on course with your past decisions by automatically deriving rules from your AI interactions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "## Version Control"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "We recommend keeping this directory under version control to maintain a history of your AI interactions. However, if you prefer not to version these files, you can exclude them by adding this to your &#96;.gitignore&#96;:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": ".specstory/**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "We recommend __not__ keeping the &#96;.specstory/ai_rules_backups&#96; directory under version control if you are already using git to version your AI rules, and committing regularly. You can exclude it by adding this to your &#96;.gitignore&#96;:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 35
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.specstory/.what-is-this.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.15320910513401031
    },

          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "The user is getting an error when running &#96;xc&#96; (which seems to run &#96;xtask git commit&#96;). The error says that &#96;xtask/src/lib.rs&#96; has 750 lines (max 500) and needs to be split into smaller modules or files."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "I need to:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "1. Read the current xtask/src/lib.rs file to understand its structure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "2. Split it into smaller modules (each under 500 lines)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "3. Update the main lib.rs to re-export or use the new modules"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "Let me start by reading the lib.rs file to understand its structure."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "ć­Łĺ¨čŻťĺ &#96;xtask/src/lib.rs&#96; çĺŽć´ĺĺŽšäťĽäşč§Łçťćďźéĺćĺä¸şĺ¤ä¸Şĺ°äş 500 čĄçć¨Ąĺă"
          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eTool use: **read_file_v2** â˘ Read file: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/xtask_todo/xtask/src/lib.rs\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {

          },

            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "I have the full lib.rs (751 lines). I need to split it into modules so each file is under 500 lines. Logical split:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "1. **lib.rs** - Entry point, XtaskCmd/XtaskSub, run/run_with, and re-exports. Keep it minimal (~100 lines)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "2. **clippy.rs** - ClippyArgs, status_to_result, cmd_clippy (~40 lines)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "3. **git.rs** - GitArgs, GitSub, GitAddArgs, GitCommitArgs, cmd_git (~60 lines)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "4. **run.rs** - RunArgs, cmd_run (~15 lines) - could merge with lib or clippy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "5. **todo.rs** (in xtask - but there's already a crate named todo!) - So I'll name the module something like &#96;todo_cmd.rs&#96; or &#96;task_todo.rs&#96; to avoid confusion. Actually in Rust we use &#96;mod todo&#96; and the module would be &#96;xtask::todo&#96; which is different from the crate &#96;todo&#96;. So &#96;mod todo&#96; is fine - the module is &#96;xtask::todo&#96;, the crate is &#96;todo&#96;. So I'll create:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "   - **todo.rs** (module) - TodoArgs, TodoSub, TodoAddArgs, etc., TodoDto, todo_file, load_todos, save_todos, format_time_ago, format_duration, is_old_open, AGE_THRESHOLD_DAYS, print_todo_list_items, cmd_todo. That's a lot - roughly lines 144-371 = 228 lines."
          }
        ],

          },

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12824,
            "text": "\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eTool use: **read_file_v2** â˘ Read file: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/xtask_todo/crates/todo/Cargo.toml\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12825
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12826,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12827,
            "text": "\u003c/tool-use\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12828
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12829,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12830
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12831,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12832
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12833,
            "text": "Now I have a clearer picture. The binary has:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12834,
            "text": "- main.rs - CLI entry, load VFS, run REPL"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12835,
            "text": "- parser.rs - tokenize, parse Pipeline/SimpleCommand (pure logic, easy to unit test if we can run it)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12836,
            "text": "- serialization.rs - load/save .bin format (pure logic + I/O)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12837,
            "text": "- vfs.rs - virtual filesystem (Node, Vfs)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12838,
            "text": "- command.rs - run_builtin_*, ExecContext (needs VFS, streams)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12839,
            "text": "- repl.rs - read loop, dispatch to command"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12840,
            "text": "- completion.rs - rustyline completion"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12841,
            "text": "- todo_io.rs - load/save todos in devshell context"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12842
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12843,
            "text": "The key insight: **cargo test -p xtask-todo-lib** only builds and runs the **lib** and **integration tests**. The integration tests (in crates/todo/tests/) are separate binaries that link against the **lib**. So the binary (cargo_devshell) code is never linked into any test binary - it's only linked when you build the cargo-devshell binary. So when tarpaulin runs tests, the binary's code is never executed. That's why coverage is 21.77% (only the lib is exercised)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12844
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12845,
            "text": "To get the binary code executed during &#96;cargo test&#96;, we have two approaches:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 12846
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 12786
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.specstory/history/2026-03-11_01-15Z-coverage.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.15182049572467804
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1215,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1216
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1217,
            "text": "_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1218
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1219,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1220
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1221,
            "text": "The user wants to add a new cargo xtask command: &#96;cargo xtask git add&#96; that runs the equivalent of &#96;git add .github .specstory xtask docs&#96;. So I need to:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1222
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1223,
            "text": "1. Add a new subcommand - but \"git add\" has a space. In argh, subcommands are typically single words. So we could do:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1224,
            "text": "   - &#96;xtask git-add&#96; (one subcommand)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1225,
            "text": "   - or &#96;xtask git&#96; with a nested &#96;add&#96; subcommand"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1226
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1227,
            "text": "   The user said \"cargo xtask git add\" - so they want the invocation to be &#96;cargo xtask git add&#96;. That means we need a \"git\" top-level subcommand with an \"add\" subcommand under it. Let me check - in argh we can have nested subcommands. So:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1228,
            "text": "   - XtaskSub::Git(GitArgs) "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1229,
            "text": "   - GitArgs has sub: GitSub"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1230,
            "text": "   - GitSub::Add(AddArgs) "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1231,
            "text": "   Then cmd would be &#96;cargo xtask git add&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1232
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1233,
            "text": "   Or we could have a single subcommand \"git-add\" and the user runs &#96;cargo xtask git-add&#96;. The user specifically said \"cargo xtask git add\" so I'll implement nested: &#96;git&#96; with subcommand &#96;add&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1234
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1235,
            "text": "2. The \"add\" subcommand would run &#96;git add .github .specstory xtask docs&#96; - so we need to execute that as a process. We need to run from the workspace root. We can use Command::new(\"git\").args([\"add\", \".github\", \".specstory\", \"xtask\", \"docs\"]).status()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1236
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1237,
            "text": "Let me read the current main.rs to see the exact structure and add the git add command."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1238,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1239
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1240,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1241
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1242
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1243
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1244
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1245
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1246,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1247
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1248,
            "text": "\u003ctool-use data-tool-type=\"read\" data-tool-name=\"read_file_v2\"\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1249,
            "text": "\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eTool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/xtask_todo/xtask/src/main.rs\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1250
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1251,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1252,
            "text": "\u003c/tool-use\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1253
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1254,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1255
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1256,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1257
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1258,
            "text": "I need to add:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1259,
            "text": "1. XtaskSub::Git(GitArgs)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1260,
            "text": "2. GitArgs with a subcommand GitSub"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1261,
            "text": "3. GitSub::Add(GitAddArgs) - can be empty struct"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1262,
            "text": "4. cmd_git that matches on GitSub::Add and runs git add .github .specstory xtask docs"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1263
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1264,
            "text": "Let me add the enum variant, the Git args struct, and the handler."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1265,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"
          },
          {
